lesson.menu_desc: Choosing gifts with this, that, these & those -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Shopping for Souvenirs: Choosing Gifts with this, that, these & those
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Roberto Pires | Aula 14 -- Shopping for Souvenirs

listenings.text: Good afternoon, and welcome to our shop. Let me show you a few souvenirs from this city. This small box on the counter is handmade, and it is a lovely gift. Those scarves over there come in many colors, and they are a famous local brand. If you buy two, you get a small discount. These postcards near the door are very cheap, and we can wrap any gift for you for free. Just ask if you would like to try on a hat or check a different size. Take your time and enjoy your visit.

I love buying souvenirs when I travel, but I learned to be careful. The first time, I bought a big gift that did not fit in my suitcase. Now I always ask three things before I pay. I ask if there is a discount, if they can wrap the gift, and how much change I will get. I prefer handmade things, not a famous brand, because they tell the story of the place. A good souvenir is small, light, and special. That is the best way to take a piece of the trip home with me.

extra_audio.text: First, Roberto walks into the souvenir shop and looks at the gifts on the shelves. Then he asks the shopkeeper about a handmade box and the price. Next, he tries on a hat and checks a different size. After that, he asks for a discount and the shopkeeper wraps the gift. Finally, Roberto pays, gets his change, and takes his souvenirs home.
